title: Macro arguments should avoid containing preprocessing directives
description: Macro arguments should not contain preprocessing directivesAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalmaintainabilityreliabilityBugpitfallbased-on-misraWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoThe preprocessor performs two main tasks: Handling preprocessor directives and expanding macros. Trying to do both simultaneously can be problematic: when a macro argument contains something that could be interpreted as a preprocessor directive, the behavior is undefined.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAvailable Since10.7©2025SonarSource Sàrl. All rights reserved.Privacy Policy|Cookie Policy|Terms of Use
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
#define PRINT(x) printf(x)
PRINT(#ifdef DEBUG
"Debug mode"
#else
"Release mode"
#endif); // Noncompliant: preprocessor directive in macro argument

#define CONCAT(a, b) a ## b
CONCAT(value, #define SUFFIX _test); // Noncompliant: #define within macro argument
compliant:
#ifdef DEBUG
    #define PRINT(x) printf(x)
#else
    #define PRINT(x) printf("Release: " x)
#endif

PRINT("Hello World"); // Compliant: preprocessor logic outside macro call

#define CONCAT(a, b) a ## b
#define SUFFIX _test
CONCAT(value, SUFFIX); // Compliant: macro defined separately

checks.message: Macro arguments should avoid containing preprocessing directives as they can lead to unexpected behavior.
